title: Built my own tool to turn trading ideas into backtest reports!
author: DeepParticular8251
selftext:
I have been building a tool for myself (I call it Coin AI) which turns plain English trading ideas into backtest reports, without having to write any code.

The problem I kept running into was that I would have a trading idea, but testing it properly meant writing code, setting up data, defining the rules, running the backtest, and then manually looking through the results. I wanted a faster way to go from idea → backtest → report → refine.

So I built a backtest engine where I can type a strategy in plain English and get a report back.

Here is a demo of me using the backtest engine:

[Demo: Coin AI turning a plain-English prompt into a Bitcoin RSI backtest report.](https://reddit.com/link/1tt0y42/video/imwgqvm38i4h1/player)

Right now it generates things like a performance summary, equity curve, trades, drawdowns, and a strategy breakdown.

I’m not trying to build a magic AI trading bot or claim that this finds alpha. I’m building this because I personally want a faster way to research strategies, compare ideas, understand risk, and avoid relying on gut feeling before testing something.

Long term, I want it to become more of a strategy research engine: research assets, test ideas, compare results, refine assumptions, and eventually paper trade or deploy strategies with risk controls.

Some things I’m thinking about adding next:

* clearer fee/slippage assumptions
* walk-forward or out-of-sample testing
* benchmark comparison
* strategy comparison
* more assets like stocks, ETFs, prediction markets, options, and futures
* a way to inspect or edit the exact rules the AI created
* better explanations of why a strategy worked or failed

I mainly built this for myself, but if other people have the same problem and want to try it, I’m happy to let people use it for free.

I’d love feedback on:

* Would you use something like this?
* What would you need to see to trust the backtest?
* What assumptions should always be visible?
* What feels missing from the demo?

If you have any questions feel free to ask!

comments.author: Whakomatic
comments.body: Hard to comment without being able to see it in action. Is there a site or a GitHub ?

comments.author: brt100
comments.body: Yes, interested, would like to try it, thx

replies.author: DeepParticular8251
replies.body: Great! Just sent you a DM

comments.author: Internal-Creme-3255
comments.body: Same I think this pretty cool

comments.author: BassDripz
comments.body: I had this exact same idea just yesterday! I probably would have never built though, I assume it will need a fair bit of tuning to get it off the ground.  It def is a great use-case for AI  though. Well done.

replies.author: DeepParticular8251
replies.body: Yeah it definitely took a lot of time to build the pipeline and fine tune the models to behave correctly. Would you be willing to test and try it out?

replies.author: BassDripz
replies.body: For sure. I can test it against some of my formal back-testing modules and compare.

comments.author: Think_Soft2282
comments.body:
honestly the demo looks cool but i've been burned enough times that i'm kind of skeptical of anything that looks clean in backtesting... like i had a strategy last year that was printing on paper and then just immediately died when i tried it live. idk if that's slippage or me being an idiot or both

i use Trade Quo mostly, global online broker offering CFD trading across a bunch of markets, and even just watching my actual fills vs what i expected is kind of sobering

anyway not trying to dump on what you built, the plain english → rules thing is genuinely the part i'd find useful, i just want to be able to see exactly what rules it generated because that's usually where the weird assumptions are hiding. like does it assume you can fill at close price or

comments.author: PleasantSomewhere990
comments.body: Being able to inspect the exact generated rules feels like the big trust unlock

comments.author: yyosefi
comments.body: I would love to test it
